mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and payroll employment indicate a coordinated demand-labor contraction that depresses aggregate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, with t-test p > 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when unemployment exceeds 8.0%, positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals a stronger-than-expected recovery from deep recession, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following positive GDPC1 surprises are negative or flat when UNRATE > 8% over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly captures consumer spending momentum, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after month-over-month RSAFS increases are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation above 3% triggers expectations of tighter future monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative after CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts combined with a rising federal funds rate signals that restrictive monetary policy is constraining interest-rate-sensitive sectors, amplifying recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST declines while FEDFUNDS rises over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 jobs indicates accelerating labor market absorption, boosting consumption forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases > 200k are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
